trends.name: Quiet Luxury 2.0
trends.description: The stealth wealth trend evolves — less logo-free basics, more artisanal craft and visible quality. Think hand-stitched details, unusual fabrics, and pieces that reward close inspection.

trends.name: Burgundy Everything
trends.description: The color of the season across all categories — from deep oxblood leather to bright cherry knits. The new neutral.

trends.name: Soft Power Tailoring
trends.description: Oversized, unstructured, gender-fluid tailoring. Suits that look like you borrowed them from someone cooler. Deconstructed shoulders, extra-wide legs, draped lapels.

trends.name: Outdoor Gorpcore Refined
trends.description: Trail gear meets city style — but elevated. Technical fabrics in sophisticated colors. Performance materials cut like fashion pieces.

trends.name: Neo Bohemia
trends.description: Boho returns with a sharper edge — less festival, more artisan. Crochet, macramé, and handcraft with clean modern silhouettes.

trends.name: Digital Nostalgia
trends.description: Y2K meets workwear — low-rise hints, cargo details, and tech-inspired materials but executed with adult sophistication.

trends.name: Butter Yellow
trends.description: Soft, warm yellows replace the acid lemon of previous seasons. Think sunlight, not highlighter. Especially stunning on deep and olive skin tones.

trends.name: Texture Play
trends.description: Mixing textures is the new color blocking — leather with knit, suede with silk, denim with cashmere. The outfit is about touch.

trends.name: Sheer Confidence
trends.description: Transparent and semi-sheer layers worn boldly — not as lingerie but as structure. Sheer knits, organza layers, mesh panels.

trends.name: The New Prep
trends.description: Ivy League codes remixed for a diverse, modern audience. Rugby stripes, cricket sweaters, and loafers — but looser, cooler, and less exclusionary.
